pi_items.source: Task description explicitly states: 'Save the file as /home/ga/Documents/LibreCAD/radiant_heating_layout.dxf'
Task description: 'Draw a room boundary rectangle 3000mm x 2500mm'
Task description: 'Draw a room boundary rectangle 3000mm x 2500mm'
Task description: 'The tubing must maintain a 150mm offset from all walls'
Calculated from room dimensions (3000x2500), wall offset (150mm), and run spacing (200mm). A serpentine pattern with ~13 runs yields ~24-26 vertices. Min of 22 provides reasonable tolerance.
Calculated from room dimensions (3000x2500), wall offset (150mm), and run spacing (200mm). A serpentine pattern with ~13 runs yields ~24-26 vertices. Max of 26 is consistent with expected geometry.
Task description: 'on layer WALLS (White)'. Verified in verifier.py line 92-93 checks for layers named WALLS, Walls, or walls.
Task description: 'on layer HEATING (Red)'. Verified in verifier.py line 93-94 checks for layers named HEATING, Heating, or heating.

privileged_info_summary: The task creates a radiant floor PEX heating layout in LibreCAD with a 3000mm x 2500mm room boundary on the WALLS layer. A continuous serpentine heating loop is drawn on the HEATING layer with 150mm offset from all walls and 200mm spacing between runs, starting at (150, 150) and ending at (150, 2350). The output file is saved to /home/ga/Documents/LibreCAD/radiant_heating_layout.dxf. The expected heating loop geometry should contain 22-26 vertices when completed as a polyline.
